sections.purpose: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI to every Indian resident. It is used as: (1) Universal identity and address proof accepted across India for government and private services. (2) KYC (Know Your Customer) for banks, mobile SIM cards, and financial services. (3) Authentication for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) — government subsidies sent directly to beneficiaries. (4) Linking with PAN, bank accounts, and Voter ID for services. (5) Basis for e-KYC for digital verification. (6) Doorstep delivery of government services. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.eligibility: Any person residing in India is eligible for Aadhaar — including Indian citizens, NRIs, and foreign nationals residing in India for 182 days or more. No age limit — newborns can be enrolled. No income, caste, or religion criteria. Each person can have only one Aadhaar number (biometrics ensure uniqueness). Source: uidai.gov.in
documents: Proof of Identity (any one): Passport, PAN Card, Voter ID (EPIC), Driving Licence, Government photo ID card, Ration Card with photo

Proof of Address (any one): Passport, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Government-issued address certificate, Electricity/Gas/Water/Telephone bill (not older than 3 months), Bank account statement or passbook, Post Office passbook, Property tax receipt, Domicile/Residence Certificate issued by Tehsildar/MRO
Proof of Date of Birth (any one): Birth Certificate, SSLC/Matriculation Certificate, Passport, PAN Card, Government-issued certificate
For children below 5: Parent/guardian's Aadhaar + child's Birth Certificate (no biometrics for children below 5)
For address update online: Valid address proof document must be uploaded on myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in
sections.fees: New Aadhaar Enrolment: FREE — no charges. (Source: uidai.gov.in — 'Aadhaar enrolment is totally free of cost'). Demographic update (Name/Address/DOB/Gender) at Aadhaar Seva Kendra: ₹50. Online Address Update via myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in: FREE (charges waived till 14 June 2027 as per UIDAI circular). Mobile number update at centre: FREE. Biometric update at centre: ₹100. PVC Aadhaar card reprint: ₹50. e-Aadhaar download: FREE. Email update via Aadhaar mobile app: FREE till 31 December 2026. Mandatory Biometric Update (MBU) for children aged 7–15: FREE (waived for 1 year w.e.f. 1 October 2025). Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.processingTime: New enrolment: Aadhaar number generated within 90 days of enrolment (typically 15–30 days). e-Aadhaar available for download within 24–48 hours after generation. Demographic updates: reflected in database within 7–10 working days. Mobile number update: linked within 24–48 hours. PVC card: delivered via Speed Post in 15–20 days. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.validity: Aadhaar has no expiry date. It is valid for life. UIDAI recommends updating documents in Aadhaar at least once every 10 years from the date of generation. Biometric update is also recommended every 10 years (optional, except for children). Source: uidai.gov.in
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Find nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra: Visit appointments.uidai.gov.in to locate a centre and book an appointment (walk-in also accepted at most centres)
Step 2 — Carry original documents: Proof of Identity, Proof of Address, and Proof of Date of Birth (as per UIDAI document list)
Step 3 — Fill the Enrolment Form at the centre
Step 4 — Biometrics captured: fingerprints (all 10), iris scan, and photograph
Step 5 — Receive Enrolment Acknowledgement Slip (EAS) with 28-digit Enrolment ID (EID) — save this for tracking
Step 6 — Aadhaar generated within 90 days — download e-Aadhaar at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using your EID or mobile number
sections.mobileUpdate: Mobile number update can ONLY be done by visiting an Aadhaar Seva Kendra in person — it cannot be done online. Carry your Aadhaar card and any valid ID proof. Biometric authentication is done at the centre. New mobile number is linked within 24–48 hours. This service is free of charge. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.addressUpdate: Online: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Update Address → Enter OTP on registered mobile → Upload valid address proof document → Submit. Free of charge (charges waived till 14 June 2027). Takes 7–10 working days. Offline: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with address proof document. Fee: ₹50. UIDAI recommends updating address whenever you move. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.nameCorrection: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with a valid document showing the correct name (Passport, PAN, School Certificate). Fee: ₹50. Processing: 7–10 working days. Note: UIDAI allows only 2 name changes per lifetime. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.dobCorrection: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with valid DOB proof (Birth Certificate, SSLC Certificate, Passport). Fee: ₹50. Processing: 7–10 working days. Note: Only 1 DOB change allowed per lifetime. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.biometricUpdate: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra for biometric update (fingerprints, iris scan, photograph). Fee: ₹100. Recommended every 10 years or when biometrics are degraded (elderly/labour workers). Children aged 7–15: Mandatory Biometric Update (MBU) is free till 30 September 2026. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.eAadhaar: e-Aadhaar is the official digital/electronic version of Aadhaar card. Download free at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. Password: first 4 letters of your name in CAPITALS + birth year (e.g., RAJE1990). Legally equivalent to physical Aadhaar card. Accepted for all purposes including banking, travel, and government services. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.pvcAadhaar: PVC Aadhaar is a credit-card-sized durable plastic version. Order at resident.uidai.gov.in. Fee: ₹50. Delivered via Speed Post in 15–20 days. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.lostAadhaar: If Aadhaar card is lost: (1) Your 12-digit Aadhaar number never changes — only the physical card is lost. (2) Download e-Aadhaar free at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in. (3) Order PVC reprint for ₹50. (4) If you forgot your number: use 'Retrieve EID/UID' at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using your registered mobile or email. (5) If mobile is not linked: visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with biometric verification. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.childAadhaar: Children below 5: enrolled with parent's Aadhaar + child's Birth Certificate. Blue-coloured Aadhaar (Baal Aadhaar) issued — no fingerprints or iris for under-5. Must update biometrics when child turns 5 (Mandatory Biometric Update 1 — MBU1) and again at 15 (MBU2). MBU for children aged 7–15 is FREE till 30 September 2026. Source: uidai.gov.in
sections.tracking: Check Aadhaar update/enrolment status: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Check Aadhaar Update Status. Enter your Update Request Number (URN) or Enrolment ID (EID). Alternatively call UIDAI helpline: 1947 (toll-free). Source: uidai.gov.in
commonMistakes: Using unofficial websites claiming to update Aadhaar — only use uidai.gov.in or myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in (Source: UIDAI warning)
Trying to update mobile number online — mobile update can ONLY be done at Aadhaar Seva Kendra in person (Source: uidai.gov.in)
Not booking appointment before visiting — leads to long wait times
Sharing Aadhaar OTP with anyone — UIDAI warns never to share OTP with strangers or unofficial agents
Not saving the Enrolment ID (EID) or Update Request Number (URN) after visiting the centre
Submitting documents that don't match the update being requested

edgeCases.title: Mobile number not linked — cannot do online services
edgeCases.problem: Person's mobile number is not linked to Aadhaar. They cannot receive OTP for any online Aadhaar services.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Visit any Aadhaar Seva Kendra in person with your Aadhaar and valid ID proof. Request mobile number update. Biometric verification done at centre. New number linked within 24–48 hours. Free of charge.

edgeCases.title: Forgot Aadhaar number
edgeCases.problem: Person lost their Aadhaar card and does not remember the 12-digit number.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Retrieve Lost or Forgotten EID/UID. Enter registered mobile or email. OTP verification reveals your Aadhaar number. If mobile not linked: visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra for biometric verification.

faqs.question: What is Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI to every Indian resident based on biometric and demographic data. It serves as universal identity and address proof across India. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: Is Aadhaar enrolment free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Aadhaar enrolment is totally free of cost. You do not need to pay anything at the enrolment centre. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to update address in Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Online (Free): Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Update Address → OTP on registered mobile → Upload address proof. Takes 7–10 days. Offline: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with address proof. Fee: ₹50. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to update mobile number in Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Mobile number can ONLY be updated by visiting an Aadhaar Seva Kendra in person. It cannot be done online. Biometric verification required. Free of charge. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: What is the fee for Aadhaar update?
faqs.answer: New enrolment: FREE. Online address update: FREE (waived till June 2027). Demographic update at centre: ₹50. Biometric update: ₹100. PVC card reprint: ₹50. e-Aadhaar download: FREE. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: Does Aadhaar expire?
faqs.answer: No. Aadhaar has no expiry date and is valid for life. UIDAI recommends updating your documents in Aadhaar at least once every 10 years. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to download e-Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Download Aadhaar → Enter Aadhaar number or EID → OTP verification → Download. Free. Password: first 4 letters of name in CAPS + birth year. Legally equivalent to physical Aadhaar. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: What if I lost my Aadhaar card?
faqs.answer: Your Aadhaar number never changes. Download e-Aadhaar free at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in, or order PVC reprint for ₹50. Forgot number? Use 'Retrieve EID/UID' on the portal. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to correct name in Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra with valid proof of correct name (Passport/PAN/School Certificate). Fee: ₹50. Processing: 7–10 days. Only 2 name changes allowed per lifetime. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to check Aadhaar update status?
faqs.answer: Visit myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in → Check Aadhaar Update Status → Enter URN or EID. Or call UIDAI helpline 1947. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: What documents are needed for Aadhaar enrolment?
faqs.answer: Proof of Identity + Proof of Address + Proof of DOB — any government-issued document (Passport, Voter ID, Driving Licence, PAN, Ration Card, Electricity Bill, etc.). Full list at uidai.gov.in. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: Where is the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra?
faqs.answer: Find and book appointment at nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra: appointments.uidai.gov.in. UIDAI helpline: 1947 (toll-free). Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How to get PVC Aadhaar card?
faqs.answer: Order PVC Aadhaar at resident.uidai.gov.in. Fee: ₹50. Delivered via Speed Post in 15–20 days. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: What if my mobile is not linked to Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Visit any Aadhaar Seva Kendra in person with your Aadhaar and valid ID proof. Mobile number linked after biometric verification. Free. Online services cannot be used until mobile is linked. Source: uidai.gov.in

faqs.question: How long does Aadhaar take to come?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar generated within 90 days of enrolment (typically 15–30 days). e-Aadhaar available for download within 24–48 hours of generation. PVC card: 15–20 days. Source: uidai.gov.in
